title: Hide Subitems from Cards View Widget
excerpt: Hi! Is there a way to hide sub-item cards from the cards view widget on dashboards? I only utilize the sub-items to link supplemental information back to the main item/pulse. So, in the cards view widget at the moment it…

posts.author: leyla (Leyla Abotteen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! Is there a way to hide sub-item cards from the cards view widget on dashboards? I only utilize the sub-items to link supplemental information back to the main item/pulse. So, in the cards view widget at the moment it’s creating unnecessary duplicate card views [One for the main item, and then a separate card view for the sub-item].

Thanks!

posts.author: nataliaz (natalia)
posts.cookedHtml: Can someone pls respond here? +1 as there’s no need for sub-items in cards view. Thx!

posts.author: tmanee (Tyler Manee)
posts.cookedHtml: Any help here? I’m having the same problem with unnecessary subitem cards.

posts.author: TeamEasy (Easy Mondays)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @tmanee,

It’s one of the reasons we tend to stay away from subitems where possible. Unfortunately, they’re much less capable and have a few quirky limitations, like not being able to delete them once added, and in this case, not being able to filter or remove them out from the cards view or cards widget on a dashboard.

However, if you find yourself stuck in how to build your workflow into the platform and wishing for someone who can understand what you’re trying to achieve and either show you how or do it for you -

Send us a message or reach out directly:

teameasy@easy-mondays.com

Team Easy

posts.author: DaveThinks (David Goodwin)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would love an answer to this one! We’re using items for “communications deliverables,” and subitems for phases such as print and delivery deadlines. I really want to report on the total number of deliverables, not each page of each deliverable.

So far subitems have worked almost perfectly - this is the first task I’ve found with no obvious solution.
